_description: Formal schema for the Economy dimension of a Living Chapter location. Backs NPC motivation_states that involve production, trade, shortages, and prices. The village economy is NOT a shop interface — it is a living system that NPCs participate in independently of the player. Wang Qingyue's motivations (trade_with_merchant, share_rumor) are the social layer; this schema is the structural layer that determines WHAT is available, HOW MUCH it costs, WHY shortages exist, and WHAT happens when the economy shifts. Per Article II (Reality First): the economy exists because it exists, not because the player needs items.

_design_principles.no_player_shop: There is no 'village shop' UI. Items exist in the world. Wang Qingyue trades what she has. The player acquires things through interaction, not through a menu.
_design_principles.npc_participation: NPCs are the primary economic actors. They produce, trade, hoard, and consume. The player is a secondary participant who may buy, sell, barter, or steal.
_design_principles.shortage_drives_motivation: When a resource drops below threshold, NPC motivations activate (Zhou Tingsu needs herbs, Wang Tianshui needs firewood). This is how economy drives social simulation.
_design_principles.seasonal_cycles: Production, consumption, and trade follow seasonal patterns. Autumn = harvest. Winter = scarcity. Spring = planting. Summer = growth.
_design_principles.teng_family_tax: The Teng family's annual tax collection is the economy's primary stressor. It removes half the rice harvest, creating a permanent structural shortage that drives NPC behavior year-round.
_design_principles.memory_coupling: Price changes, shortages, and trade deals write to the memory ledger. NPCs remember when the price of salt tripled or when the harvest failed.

schema.location_id: string — must match a Living Chapter location

currency.primary: string — the main medium of exchange (wang_family_village: 'barter + rice')
secondary: string — secondary currencies (spirit_stones, salt, iron, wolf_pelts)

exchange_rates._note: Exchange rates are NOT fixed. They fluctuate based on supply, demand, season, and events.

base_rates.base_ratio: float — baseline exchange ratio (e.g. 1 wolf_pelt = 3 salt)
base_rates.volatility: float 0-1 — how much this rate fluctuates per season (0.1 = stable, 0.8 = volatile)

resources.type: enum — food | material | medicine | tool | luxury | cultivation

production.rate_per_season: float — base production per season (in abstract units)

seasonal_modifier.spring: float multiplier (e.g. 0.3 — planting, no harvest yet)
seasonal_modifier.summer: float multiplier (e.g. 0.8 — growth, some early harvest)

producers: string — npc_ids who produce this resource

event_modifiers.duration_days: int — how long the modifier lasts

consumption.base_rate_per_day: float — how much the village consumes per day

seasonal_modifier.winter: float (typically higher — more fuel needed, less activity)

stockpile.current: float — current amount in village stores
stockpile.decay_rate: float per day — spoilage (food) or degradation (medicine/herbs)

resources.shortage_threshold: float — when stockpile drops below this, shortage motivations activate on relevant NPCs
resources.surplus_threshold: float — when stockpile exceeds this, trade/sale motivations activate

trade_value.supply_demand_factor: float — price adjusts: actual = base * (surplus_threshold / max(current, 0.1))

npc_markup.markup_percent: int — Wang Qingyue marks up 15%; the Teng family demands 30% below market

trade_routes.name: string — e.g. 'South Road Merchant Caravan'

event_modifiers.effect: string — e.g. 'frequency *= 0.5' (bandit activity on the road)

trade_routes.npc_handler: string — which NPC manages this trade route (wang_qingyue)

taxation.collector: string — faction or NPC who collects (teng_family)
taxation.rate: float — fraction of production taken (0.5 = half the harvest)

consequences.on_failure_to_pay: string — e.g. 'teng_family.enforcement_action triggers; violence or forced labor'

hidden_economy._note: Wang Tianshui hides 1/10 of the grain. This is his m_resist_teng_family_quietly motivation in data form.
hidden_economy.discovery_probability_per_tax: float — chance the Teng family finds it each collection (0.05 per year)

seasonal_cycle._note: The economy follows a seasonal pattern that repeats every 4 seasons. Each season has distinct production, consumption, and trade characteristics.

seasons.name: spring
seasons.npc_motivation_shift: Wang Tianshui: irrigation repair. Zhou Tingsu: herb garden planting. Da Niu: field preparation.

seasons.name: summer
seasons.npc_motivation_shift: Zhou Rui: active hunting. Zhou Tingsu: herb gathering (peak). Wang Qingyue: trade with passing caravans.

seasons.name: autumn
seasons.npc_motivation_shift: ALL NPCs: harvest urgency. Wang Qingyue: negotiate for salt and iron. Wang Tianshui: Teng family tax preparation. Hidden grain storage.

seasons.name: winter
seasons.npc_motivation_shift: Wang Tianshui: winter readiness (firewood, grain check). Zhou Rui: ice fishing. Zhang Tian: more drinking. Villagers: survival mode.

economic_events._note: Economic events are events that directly affect the economy. They link to living_events via event_id.

wang_family_village_instance._comment: Seed data for Wang Family Village. This is the canonical Gold-Standard Economy implementation. Other villages, sects, and cities will have their own instances.

resources._comment: Spirit stones are not part of the village economy. They appear only when cultivators visit. The village has zero. This is intentional — it shows the gap between mortal and cultivator economies.

trade_routes.name: South Road Merchant Caravan
trade_routes.canon_fidelity: INFERRED — mortal villages trade with passing caravans; specific route is inferred.

consequences.on_failure_to_pay: teng_family.enforcement_action triggers; violence or forced labor

_how_this_satisfies_gold_standard.what_this_provides: The formal data structure behind Wang Qingyue's trade motivation and Wang Tianshui's winter readiness motivation. Defines 7 resources with production/consumption/stockpile/shortage/surplus thresholds. 1 trade route. 1 taxation system with hidden economy. Seasonal cycle across all 4 seasons. 3 economic events linked to living_events. The village economy now has structure that NPC motivations reference and that Java will wire into WorldEventBus and IntentEngine.
_how_this_satisfies_gold_standard.what_remains: Java wiring (JDK-blocked). The economy_schema is data — the IntentEngine already reads motivation_states and produces intents; this schema defines the WORLD STATE that those motivations evaluate against.
